ask_the_ai_tutor_a_question: Ask the AI tutor a question
what_number_does_the_arrow_point_to: What number does the arrow point to?
estimate_the_value_the_arrow_points_to: Estimate the value the arrow points to (nearest whole number).
click_the_number_line_above_to_place_a: Click the number line above to place a marker.
number_line_marker_use_arrow_keys_to_m: Number line marker. Use arrow keys to move, Home/End for endpoints, Shift for larger steps.
number_line_fractions_above_decimals_b: 📏 Number line — fractions above, decimals below
same_amount_three_more_visuals_quantit: 🔄 Same amount, three more visuals. Quantity is conserved across every representation.
move_the_marker_by_a_fraction_fraction: ➕➖ Move the marker by a fraction — fraction arithmetic on the line
click_a_chip_below_the_marker_slides_a: Click a chip below. The marker slides; all four visuals update. The line auto-extends if you drift past the edge.
common_denominator_hunter_how_to_add_f: 🤝 Common-denominator hunter — how to add fractions with different denominators
you_cannot_add_1_2_1_3_directly_first_: You cannot add 1/2 + 1/3 directly. First find a shared denominator (the LCM works), rename each fraction, then add the numerators.
plot_the_sum_on_the_line: → Plot the sum on the line
equivalent_fractions_same_point_many_n: ∞ Equivalent fractions — same point, many names. Click to re-divide the line.
multiply_or_divide_the_top_and_bottom_: Multiply (or divide) the top AND bottom by the same number → same value, different name. The marker does not move.
a_quarter_is_0_25_1_4_of_a_dollar_a_di: A quarter is $0.25 = 1/4 of a dollar. A dime is $0.10 = 1/10. Coins are decimal-fraction practice.
every_decimal_place_is_a_fraction_1st_: Every decimal place IS a fraction. 1st place: tenths, 2nd: hundredths, 3rd: thousandths.
reset_frac_dec_tab_to_defaults: Reset Frac Dec tab to defaults
why_fractions_and_decimals_are_the_sam: 💡 Why fractions and decimals are the same thing
a_fraction_is_a_division_problem: A fraction IS a division problem.
3_4_means_3_divided_by_4_type_3_4_into: 3/4 means "3 divided by 4." Type 3 ÷ 4 into any calculator: 0.75. The slash and ÷ mean the same thing.
a_decimal_is_a_fraction_with_a_hidden_: A decimal IS a fraction with a hidden denominator.
0_6_means_6_tenths_6_10_0_25_means_25_: 0.6 means "6 tenths," 6/10. 0.25 means "25 hundredths," 25/100, which simplifies to 1/4.
the_point_at_1_4_is_the_same_point_as_: The point at 1/4 is the same point as the point at 0.25. Same spot, two names.
1_2_0_5_stops_1_4_0_25_stops_1_3_0_333: 1/2 = 0.5 (stops). 1/4 = 0.25 (stops). 1/3 = 0.333… (repeats forever). The denominator decides: if it factors into only 2s and 5s, it terminates. Otherwise it repeats. Watch the ↻ symbol on the decimal card.
focus_explore_body: Set the range, add markers, and compare distances.
focus_challenges_body: Practice estimating, rounding, and locating values.
focus_frac_body: Connect fractions, decimals, percents, and area models.
focus_compare_body: Test fraction size relationships without a score.
focus_summary: Use one line to locate values, show jumps, and connect equivalent forms.
explore_the_visual_home_for_number_sen: Explore — the visual home for number sense
numbers_as_positions_on_a_line_not_jus: Numbers as positions on a line, not just symbols. Negatives mirror across zero. Fractions sit BETWEEN integers; decimals are the same idea finer-grained. Common Core 2.MD.6, 3.NF.2, 6.NS.6.
where_does_7_8_sit_what_about_2_4_esti: Where does 7/8 sit? What about −2.4? Estimation builds magnitude sense. Studies (Siegler 2009) show number-line precision predicts later math success better than rote facts.
skip_count_the_bridge_to_multiplicatio: Skip Count — the bridge to multiplication
2_4_6_8_the_2_times_table_walking_skip: 2, 4, 6, 8… = the 2 times table walking. Skip counting by 5s and 10s pre-builds money and time. Counting backward by 3s pre-builds subtraction and division. The line shows the rhythm.
fractions_decimals_same_point_two_name: Fractions ↔ Decimals, same point, two names
a_fraction_is_a_division_problem_a_dec: A fraction is a division problem. A decimal is a fraction with a hidden denominator of 10, 100, 1000… 1/4 and 0.25 are the SAME spot on the line. Common Core 4.NF.6, 4.NF.7, 5.NBT.3.
two_fractions_four_possible_relationsh: Two fractions, four possible relationships: equal, touching the ½ landmark, A smaller, or B smaller. Sweep the sliders, log what you notice, and write your own comparison rule.
adjust_the_numerators_and_denominators: Adjust the numerators and denominators of two fractions. The widget tells you one of four discrete relationships. No score, no reveal — just sweep and notice.
hypothesis_free_text_when_do_two_fract: Hypothesis (free text): When do two fractions equal each other? When is one near ½?
find_two_fractions_with_different_nume: Find two fractions with different numerators and denominators that are equal.
what_makes_a_fraction_close_to_what_ab: What makes a fraction close to ½? What about close to 1?
when_are_two_fractions_easy_to_compare: When are two fractions easy to compare? When are they hard?
i_understand_explain_in_my_own_words: I understand — explain in my own words
explain_how_to_compare_two_fractions_w: Explain how to compare two fractions without converting to decimals.
design_note_discrete_4_state_outcome_n: Design note: discrete 4-state outcome; no fraction-comparison score; no reveal — by design.
1_4_tabs_n_new_challenge_ai_tutor_on_m: ⌨️ 1-4: tabs | N: new challenge | ?: AI tutor | ← → on marker: nudge | Home/End: ends
integers_absolute_value_distance_from_: Integers - Absolute value = distance from zero
integer_lab_summary: Open the mini lab when negative numbers are the focus.
absolute_value_number_line_showing_dis: Absolute value number line showing distance from zero
